prerequisites: A Google Cloud project containing the Cloud Storage bucket you want to read from.
The name of the target GCS bucket (the required `bucket` config field).
At least one stream defined (the required `streams` config field), identifying the file path/glob and format to sync from the bucket.

An identity (service account or user/OAuth credentials) granted an IAM role with read access to the bucket, such as Storage Object Viewer (roles/storage.objectViewer), which allows storage.objects.get and storage.objects.list.

authMethods.name: Service account key (JSON)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google Cloud service account JSON key, supplied via the `credentials.service_account` field. Create the key in the Cloud Console under IAM & Admin > Service Accounts > Keys > Add key > Create new key > JSON; the downloaded JSON contains the project ID, private key, and client email. Grant the service account a role with read access to the bucket (for example roles/storage.objectViewer).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a Google user account using OAuth 2.0, supplied via `credentials.client_id`, `credentials.client_secret`, `credentials.access_token`, and `credentials.refresh_token`. The authenticated user must hold an IAM role granting read access to the bucket, such as Storage Object Viewer (roles/storage.objectViewer).

setupSteps.title: Identify the bucket and confirm read access
setupSteps.body: Determine the GCS bucket name you want to sync from and ensure the identity you will authenticate with has read permission on it. Cloud Storage uses IAM for authorization; grant a read role such as Storage Object Viewer (roles/storage.objectViewer), which provides storage.objects.get and storage.objects.list.

setupSteps.title: Create credentials (service account JSON key)
setupSteps.body: To use a service account, open the Google Cloud Console, go to IAM & Admin > Service Accounts, select the service account, open the Keys tab, click Add key > Create new key, choose JSON, and click Create. The browser downloads the JSON key file once; store it securely because it cannot be downloaded again. Provide its contents in the `credentials.service_account` field.

setupSteps.title: Or configure OAuth 2.0 credentials
setupSteps.body: To authenticate as a user instead, provide OAuth 2.0 credentials in the `credentials` object: `client_id`, `client_secret`, `access_token`, and `refresh_token`. The authenticated user must hold an IAM role granting read access to the bucket.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the required `bucket` field to your bucket name and the `credentials` field with your chosen auth method. Define at least one entry in the required `streams` field describing the files and format to sync. Optionally set `start_date` to limit syncing to files modified after a date, and `delivery_method` and `sanitize_signed_urls` as needed.

sources.title: Create and delete service account keys (Google Cloud IAM)
IAM roles for Cloud Storage (Storage Object Viewer)
